title: Strings, Byte Buffers, Length, Capacity, and Encoding · Guided Pointer Studio
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working length-aware text and byte boundary while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of length-aware text and byte boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish C strings, unterminated character arrays, byte buffers, string literals, and encoded text.
Track logical length, allocated capacity, required terminator space, and maximum scan distance separately.
Use size-aware copying and formatting with return-value checks and truncation policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop String Buffer Distinction
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 9877. Produce bounded C evidence to preserve length, capacity, and termination invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Legacy Api Defense, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 6029. Produce bounded C evidence to preserve length, capacity, and termination invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Legacy Api Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A larger destination makes an unbounded legacy copy safe enough.

stimulus.title: Legacy Api Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A larger destination makes an unbounded legacy copy safe enough.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Legacy Api Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Legacy Api Defense case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Legacy Api Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Legacy Api Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 3696. Produce bounded C evidence to preserve length, capacity, and termination invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Legacy Api Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Legacy Api Defense, keep Legacy Api Defense connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Recognize unbounded and ambiguous string APIs as defect-analysis material and migrate them behind tested contracts.
